methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
PILGRIM, a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Jake Adler, has raised $4.3M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 10 companies across the Defense & Security sector—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Flock Safety.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $4.3M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $10.8M. Peer-median analysis draws from five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Tenna Systems ($60M, Seed), NODA AI ($125M, Series A), Ares Industries ($150M, Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M, Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M, Series A). The median across this set is $150M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $9M to $172.5M, with a median of $80M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around two factors: (1) stage risk, as stage-progression alone yields $10.8M versus peer medians anchored by Series A companies, and (2) heterogeneity within the defense-tech sector, where relative valuations span nearly an order of magnitude. Resolution of product-market fit and early revenue metrics would be critical to narrowing this range.
